  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Officer (NYSC)",
  "description": "Beux Concept is recruiting suitable and qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer (NYSC)
Job Location: Iyana-Ipaja, Lagos
Employment Type: NYSC

Job Summary


	We are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented NYSC Member currently serving in Lagos to join our finance team as an Account Officer. This role offers a great opportunity for a serving &quot;Corper&quot; to gain hands-on experience in corporate accounting and financial operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in the preparation of daily financial records and petty cash management.
	Support the senior accountant in bank reconciliations and expense tracking.
	Maintain organized digital and physical documentation of all financial transactions.
	Provide administrative support to the finance department as needed.
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles, proficiency in Microsoft Excel, and a strong willingness to learn.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	B.Sc or HND in Accounting, Finance, or a closely related field.
	NYSC Status: Must be a currently serving Corper with at least 8 months remaining in the service year.
	Candidates must reside within close proximity to any of the following areas in Ayobo, Iyana-Ipaja, Igando, Ikotun, Ikeja, Egbeda, or Abule-Egba.


Work Schedule:


	Monday &ndash; Friday: 8:00 AM &ndash; 5:00 PM
	Saturday: 8:00 AM &ndash; 1:00 PM
	Off day - On CDS days.


Salary
N60,000 - N80,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their &nbsp;CV and a copy of their NYSC Call-up letter or Green Card (to verify service duration) to: Beuxmarkethub@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Account Officer (NYSC) Job at Beux Concept




        
    

        
        Beux Concept is recruiting suitable and qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer (NYSC)
Job Location: Iyana-Ipaja, Lagos
Employment Type: NYSC

Job Summary


	We are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented NYSC Member currently serving in Lagos to join our finance team as an Account Officer. This role offers a great opportunity for a serving "Corper" to gain hands-on experience in corporate accounting and financial operations.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in the preparation of daily financial records and petty cash management.
	Support the senior accountant in bank reconciliations and expense tracking.
	Maintain organized digital and physical documentation of all financial transactions.
	Provide administrative support to the finance department as needed.
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles, proficiency in Microsoft Excel, and a strong willingness to learn.


Requirements & Qualifications


	B.Sc or HND in Accounting, Finance, or a closely related field.
	NYSC Status: Must be a currently serving Corper with at least 8 months remaining in the service year.
	Candidates must reside within close proximity to any of the following areas in Ayobo, Iyana-Ipaja, Igando, Ikotun, Ikeja, Egbeda, or Abule-Egba.


Work Schedule:


	Monday – Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
	Saturday: 8:00 AM – 1:00 PM
	Off day - On CDS days.


Salary
N60,000 - N80,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their  CV and a copy of their NYSC Call-up letter or Green Card (to verify service duration) to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager (Payroll & Compliance)",
  "description": "Medife Limited, a real estate and consulting company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager (Payroll &amp; Compliance)
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are a B2B payroll, compliance, and workforce financial wellness company helping Nigerian and UK businesses manage their people operations with less friction and more confidence.
	We sit at the intersection of fintech and HR tech&mdash;building the infrastructure that ensures people get paid correctly and businesses stay compliant.


Role Summary


	We&rsquo;re looking for a Business Development Manager who understands business and numbers&mdash;someone who can engage HR leaders, Finance teams, and founders, and clearly communicate the value of getting payroll and compliance right.
	This is not a just a &ldquo;volume sales&rdquo; role. You will sell a critical business solution, not a nice-to-have product.


What You&rsquo;ll Do


	Own the full sales cycle: prospecting &rarr; demo &rarr; negotiation &rarr; close.
	Identify and engage HR, Finance, and business decision-makers .
	Translate payroll and compliance into clear business value.
	Build and manage a strong pipeline using CRM tools
	Collaborate with product and marketing to refine messaging based on market feedback.
	Drive revenue growth by closing high-quality deals.


What Sets You Apart


	You can confidently speak the language of finance and business.
	You&rsquo;re consultative, not just transactional.
	You simplify complex ideas into clear, compelling conversations.
	You are structured, disciplined, and results-driven.


Why Join Us


	Sell a product businesses genuinely need
	Work directly with decision-makers (not just users)
	Be part of building critical infrastructure for businesses
	High ownership, high impact role.
	Competitive salary and a supportive, high-quality work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: emmykonsulting@gmail.com and copy to: meo4success@gmail.com using &quot;Business Development Manager (Payroll &amp; Compliance)&quot; as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only qualified candidates would be contacted.",

Business Development Manager (Payroll & Compliance) Job at Medife Limited




        
    

        
        Medife Limited, a real estate and consulting company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager (Payroll & Compliance)
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are a B2B payroll, compliance, and workforce financial wellness company helping Nigerian and UK businesses manage their people operations with less friction and more confidence.
	We sit at the intersection of fintech and HR tech—building the infrastructure that ensures people get paid correctly and businesses stay compliant.


Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We’re looking for a Business Development Manager who understands business and numbers—someone who can engage HR leaders, Finance teams, and founders, and clearly communicate the value of getting payroll and compliance right.
	This is not a just a “volume sales” role. You will sell a critical business solution, not a nice-to-have product.


What You’ll Do


	Own the full sales cycle: prospecting → demo → negotiation → close.
	Identify and engage HR, Finance, and business decision-makers .
	Translate payroll and compliance into clear business value.
	Build and manage a strong pipeline using CRM tools
	Collaborate with product and marketing to refine messaging based on market feedback.
	Drive revenue growth by closing high-quality deals.


What Sets You Apart


	You can confidently speak the language of finance and business.
	You’re consultative, not just transactional.
	You simplify complex ideas into clear, compelling conversations.
	You are structured, disciplined, and results-driven.


Why Join Us


	Sell a product businesses genuinely need
	Work directly with decision-makers (not just users)
	Be part of building critical infrastructure for businesses
	High ownership, high impact role.
	Competitive salary and a supportive, high-quality work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and copy to: [email protected] using "Business Development Manager (Payroll & Compliance)" as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only qualified candidates would be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

        Design Union is a real estate development group with divisions in Building design, construction, real estate development and hospitality.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cafe and Front Office Executive - Female

Job Location: Lekki / Ikate, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Operations / Hospitality

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a dual-skilled Cafe and Front Office Executive to oversee daily front-of-house operations and café services.
	This role blends guest relations, administrative coordination, and food & beverage management to ensure a seamless, welcoming experience for all visitors, clients, or guests.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of the café to ensure smooth service delivery.
	Supervise café staff, assign duties, and monitor attendance and punctuality.
	Ensure compliance with hygiene, sanitation, and food safety standards.
	Monitor stock levels, inventory, and coordinate procurement of café supplies.
	Ensure exceptional customer service standards are maintained at all times.
	Supervise daily front desk operations including check-in/check-out, switchboard, and guest inquiries.
	Manage room or workspace reservations, handle booking systems, and optimize occupancy.
	Train and lead front office staff on customer service, policies, and emergency procedures.
	Resolve guest complaints promptly, maintaining high satisfaction scores.
	Coordinate with housekeeping, security, and maintenance to ensure facility readiness.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Minimum of Bachelor’s Degree in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	4–7 years of experience in café, hospitality, restaurant, or customer service management.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong problem-solving and customer complaint resolution skills.
	Good knowledge of café operations, inventory management, and customer service standards.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
